Title: Ship To - View Saved Addresses
Preconditions: User logged in, saved addresses exist on personal account (/account/addresses is personal-only; org users see address management via /company/info)
Steps:
1. [NAV] Navigate to {{FRONT_URL}}/account/addresses
2. [WAIT] Page loads — verify address list section visible
3. [ASSERT] Verify address list displays saved addresses
4. [ASSERT] Verify each address shows: Name, Street, City, State, ZIP, Phone
5. [ASSERT] Verify default address is marked
6. [ASSERT] Verify search/filter controls visible if page has search capability (backed by currentCustomerAddresses query)
Assertions:
[DOM] Address list displays all saved addresses
[DOM] Each address shows full details (Name, Street, City, State, ZIP, Phone)
[DOM] Default address has 'Default' badge
[DOM] Edit and Delete options visible per address
[DOM] If search/keyword input present: input is accessible and functional
[DOM] If facet filters present (Country/State/City): dropdowns are rendered and selectable
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[API] currentCustomerAddresses query called on page load; errors[] is empty; totalCount matches visible item count
[CONSOLE] no uncaught JS errors
Failure_Signals: Address list empty when addresses exist; default badge missing; address details incomplete; currentCustomerAddresses errors[] non-empty on page load
References: VCST-4575; Synced TLC-2026-04-23-1700 (currentCustomerAddresses backing + UI controls)

Title: Ship To - Add New Address
Preconditions: User logged in on personal account. Address form backed by updateMemberAddresses mutation. If checkDuplicateAddress is called before submit, a warning is shown for duplicate; unique address should save without warning.
Steps:
1. [NAV] Navigate to {{FRONT_URL}}/account/addresses
2. [ACT] Click 'Add New Address'
3. [ACT] Fill form: First Name: Jane, Last Name: Doe, Address: 456 New St, City: Springfield, State: IL, ZIP: 62701, Phone: 555-987-6543
4. [ACT] Optionally check 'Set as default'
5. [ACT] Click Save
6. [WAIT] Page/form response
7. [ASSERT] Verify address saved and appears in list
Assertions:
[DOM] Add address button visible
[DOM] Form displays all required fields
[DOM] State dropdown populated based on country
[DOM] Success message: 'Address added' or equivalent toast
[DOM] New address appears in list
[DOM] Default badge shown if set as default
[DOM] If checkDuplicateAddress is wired to the form: no duplicate warning for a genuinely new address
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[API] updateMemberAddresses mutation returns HTTP 200; errors[] is empty
[API] If checkDuplicateAddress called before submit: isDuplicated = false for the new unique address; errors[] empty
[CONSOLE] no uncaught JS errors
Failure_Signals: Add address button missing; form missing required fields; address not appearing in list after save; validation not applied to ZIP or phone; duplicate warning shown for a new unique address (false positive)
References: VCST-4575; VCST-4710 (checkDuplicateAddress wired to add-address form); Synced TLC-2026-04-23-1700

Title: Ship To - XSS Prevention in Address Form Fields
Preconditions: User logged in, address form accessible
Steps: 1. [NAV] Navigate to Account > Addresses > Add New Address 2. [ACT] Fill First Name: '' 3. [ACT] Fill Last Name: 'Test' 4. [ACT] Fill Phone: '' 5. [ACT] Fill Street: '123 Main St' 6. [ACT] Fill City, State, ZIP with valid data 7. [ACT] Click Save 8. [ASSERT] Verify form rejects HTML tags in First Name — validation error shown OR tags stripped 9. [ASSERT] Verify form rejects HTML tags in Phone — validation error shown OR tags stripped 10. [ACT] Fix First Name to 'John' 11. [ACT] Enter Phone with ' 12. [ACT] Click Save 13. [ASSERT] Verify phone field rejects script tag 14. [ACT] Fix Phone to '555-123-4567' 15. [ACT] Enter Street: '
Assertions: [DOM] HTML tag payloads rejected at form level with inline validation error OR silently stripped [DOM] No
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[CONSOLE] No JS errors or XSS alert dialogs triggered
[API] updateMemberAddresses mutation returns errors[] for HTML payloads if server-side validation enabled
[DOM] Address list renders all fields as text nodes, not HTML
Failure_Signals: XSS payload accepted and saved; <script> tag rendered in address display; alert dialog fires on page load; HTML stored verbatim in phone/name fields
References: VCST-4803 coverage gap — address form XSS

Title: Ship To - Add Duplicate Address Shows Warning (Duplicate Prevention UX)
Preconditions: User logged in on personal account. User has at least one saved address. The add-address form calls checkDuplicateAddress before submitting updateMemberAddresses mutation. Backed by PR #129.
Steps:
1. [NAV] Navigate to {{FRONT_URL}}/account/addresses
2. [WAIT] Address list loads — note the first saved address fields (line1, city, country, postal code)
3. [ACT] Click 'Add New Address' button
4. [WAIT] Add address form/modal opens
5. [ACT] Fill form with the SAME address data as the existing saved address: same Street, City, Country, ZIP (copy fields exactly from list)
6. [ACT] Fill First Name, Last Name, Phone (can differ from original)
7. [ACT] Click Save (or equivalent submit action)
8. [WAIT] Form response visible
Assertions:
[DOM] Duplicate warning message visible after Save attempt — e.g. 'This address already exists' or similar
[STATE] Address NOT added to list when duplicate detected — totalCount unchanged
[DOM] If warning is a non-blocking dialog: user is offered option to cancel or proceed
[DOM] If duplicate skip is silent: no new row appears in address list; no success toast fires
[STATE] Either (a) warning shown + save blocked, OR (b) silent skip + no duplicate in list — both are valid implementations of the duplicate prevention invariant
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[API] checkDuplicateAddress called before submit — isDuplicated = true in response; errors[] empty
[API] updateMemberAddresses mutation NOT called if frontend blocks on isDuplicated=true OR called but duplicate skipped silently (totalCount unchanged)
[CONSOLE] no uncaught JS errors
Failure_Signals: Duplicate address saved without warning (duplicate prevention not working); totalCount increases after duplicate save; two identical addresses appear in list; checkDuplicateAddress not called (network tab shows no such request before submit)
Cleanup: delete any accidentally created duplicate address
References: VCST-4710 PR #129 — checkDuplicateAddress + UpdateAddresses duplicate skip; TLC-2026-04-23-1700

Title: Ship To - Address Facet Filters (Country/State/City) on /account/addresses
Preconditions: User logged in on personal account. Account has 10+ addresses across multiple countries, states, and cities. Seed data: test-data/addresses/techflow-org-addresses-state-20260423.json provides reference for multi-country data shape. Personal user must have their own seeded multi-country addresses. Use {{ORG_USER_EMAIL}} if /account/addresses is accessible for org-affiliated personal user with personal addresses.
Steps:
1. [NAV] Navigate to {{FRONT_URL}}/account/addresses
2. [WAIT] Address list loads with 10+ addresses visible
3. [ASSERT] Verify facet filter dropdowns visible: Country, State/Province, City
4. [ACT] Select 'United States of America' (or 'United States') from Country dropdown
5. [WAIT] List updates
6. [ASSERT] Verify only US addresses shown (countryCode USA or US)
7. [ACT] Select a state from State dropdown (e.g. California)
8. [WAIT] List updates
9. [ASSERT] Verify only addresses in California shown
10. [ACT] Select a city from City dropdown (e.g. San Francisco)
11. [WAIT] List updates
12. [ASSERT] Verify only San Francisco, CA, USA addresses shown
13. [ACT] Clear all facets (click reset or select 'All' in each dropdown)
14. [WAIT] List restores
15. [ASSERT] Verify full address list restored to original count
Assertions:
[DOM] Country, State/Province, City facet dropdowns visible above address list
[DOM] Country filter: only US addresses shown after selecting USA
[DOM] State filter: only California addresses shown after selecting California
[DOM] City filter: only San Francisco addresses shown after selecting San Francisco
[COUNT] Combined 3-facet result is a strict subset of each individual facet result
[DOM] Clearing all facets restores full address list
[FORMAT] City facet displays city names (e.g. 'San Francisco') NOT postal codes (e.g. '94102') — label vs term distinction
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[API] currentCustomerAddresses called with countryCodes/regionIds/cities args matching selected facets; errors[] empty
[API] term_facets in response populated with country, state, city facet entries
[CONSOLE] no uncaught JS errors
Failure_Signals: Facet dropdowns missing from /account/addresses; Country filter not narrowing list; City facet shows postal codes instead of city names (city/postal-code label mapping bug); clearing facets does not restore full list; currentCustomerAddresses errors[] non-empty with 'argument not found'
References: VCST-4710 PR #129; TechFlow seed data (22 addresses across USA/CAN/GBR); TLC-2026-04-23-1700

Title: Ship To - Address Search Keyword Filters List
Preconditions: User logged in on personal account. Account has 3+ addresses including one with a distinct city not shared by others (e.g. 'Portland'). Page backed by currentCustomerAddresses(keyword:) query.
Steps:
1. [NAV] Navigate to {{FRONT_URL}}/account/addresses
2. [WAIT] Address list loads
3. [ASSERT] Verify keyword search input visible on page
4. [ACT] Type partial city name (e.g. 'Port') into search input
5. [WAIT] List filters to matching addresses
6. [ASSERT] Verify only addresses containing 'Port' in city, street, or name visible
7. [ACT] Type full city name 'Portland' into search input
8. [WAIT] List updates
9. [ASSERT] Verify only Portland addresses shown; totalCount reduced
10. [ACT] Clear search input (backspace to empty OR click clear icon)
11. [WAIT] List restores
12. [ASSERT] Verify full address list restored (totalCount matches original)
Assertions:
[DOM] Keyword search input visible above address list
[DOM] Typing partial text narrows displayed addresses to matching items
[COUNT] totalCount after keyword filter < totalCount unfiltered (keyword is specific enough)
[DOM] Clearing search input restores full list with original count
[STATE] Keyword search is case-insensitive: 'Portland' and 'portland' return same count
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[API] currentCustomerAddresses called with keyword arg matching typed text; errors[] empty
[API] Response totalCount decreases when keyword applied
[CONSOLE] no uncaught JS errors
Failure_Signals: Keyword input missing from page; typing does not filter list; clearing does not restore list; search is case-sensitive (Portland vs portland return different counts); currentCustomerAddresses errors[] non-empty

Title: Invalid SKU Handling
Preconditions: Quick Order page open
Steps:
1. [NAV] Navigate to /bulk-order
2. [ACT] Enter a valid SKU in row 1 and confirm
3. [ACT] Enter a non-existent SKU 'FAKE-SKU-99999' in row 2 and confirm
4. [ASSERT] Verify error state on row 2
5. [ASSERT] Verify row 1 is unaffected
Assertions:
[DOM] Row 2 shows error: 'Product not found' or 'Invalid SKU'
[DOM] Row 1 with valid SKU remains displayed correctly
[DOM] Error scoped to invalid row only (other rows unaffected)
[STATE] 'Add All to Cart' skips or warns about invalid rows
Failure_Signals: Error not shown for invalid SKU; error spreading to valid rows; Add All to Cart silently skips invalid rows without warning

Title: Multi-row Entry
Preconditions: Quick Order page open
Steps:
1. [NAV] Navigate to /bulk-order
2. [ACT] Enter 5 different valid SKUs in separate rows
3. [ACT] Set quantities: 1, 3, 2, 5, 10
4. [ASSERT] Verify all 5 rows display product info correctly
5. [ASSERT] Verify running total or summary calculated correctly
Assertions:
[DOM] All 5 rows display with product name, price, and set quantity
[DOM] Page does not slow down or lose state with 5+ rows
[MATH] Running total (if displayed) sums subtotals for all rows correctly
[DOM] Can continue adding rows beyond 5
Failure_Signals: Page performance degradation with 5+ rows; row data lost when adding new rows; running total calculation incorrect

Title: Paste from Spreadsheet
Preconditions: Quick Order page open, clipboard contains tab-separated SKU and quantity data
Steps:
1. [ACT] In a text editor, create 3 rows with format: SKU[tab]Quantity
2. [ACT] Copy the 3 rows to clipboard
3. [NAV] Navigate to /bulk-order
4. [KEY] Press Ctrl+V in the bulk order entry area or first SKU field
5. [ASSERT] Verify rows auto-populated with pasted SKUs and quantities
Assertions:
[DOM] Paste action auto-populates multiple rows from tab-separated clipboard data
[DOM] SKU and quantity from each pasted row assigned to correct columns
[DOM] Product lookup triggered for each pasted SKU
[DOM] Invalid SKUs in pasted data flagged per row
[DOM] Valid products display correctly after paste
Failure_Signals: Paste not triggering row population; SKUs and quantities mixed up across columns; product lookup not triggered for pasted SKUs

Title: Bulk Order Quantity Validation
Preconditions: Quick Order page with a valid product SKU entered
Steps:
1. [NAV] Navigate to /bulk-order and enter a valid SKU
2. [ACT] Set quantity field to 0
3. [ACT] Attempt to add to cart
4. [ASSERT] Verify validation error shown
5. [ACT] Set quantity to -1 (negative)
6. [ASSERT] Verify validation error
7. [ACT] Set valid quantity: 1
8. [ASSERT] Verify add to cart succeeds
Assertions:
[DOM] Quantity 0 triggers error: 'Quantity must be at least 1'
[DOM] Negative quantity triggers validation error
[STATE] Add to Cart or Add All to Cart blocked for rows with invalid quantities
[STATE] Setting valid positive quantity clears error and enables add action
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[API] mutation errors[] is empty
[CONSOLE] no uncaught JS errors
Failure_Signals: Zero or negative quantity accepted without error; Add to Cart not blocked for invalid quantities

[STATE] Subscription persists after page refresh

Title: Unsubscribe from Alert
